description: Notification bell: add hover dropdown showing recent notifications, add notification count badge, create dedicated notifications page (not tickets), remove duplicate lang switcher from topbar
steps: 1. In panel/web/admin/src/components/TheTopbar.vue: Remove the entire .lang-switcher section (the button group with EN/FA/ZH). The sidebar already has a lang-select dropdown that works.
2. In panel/web/admin/src/components/TheTopbar.vue: Add a notification count badge (red dot with number) on the bell icon button, visible when notificationCount > 0.

3. In panel/web/admin/src/components/TheTopbar.vue: Add a hover/click dropdown (.notif-dropdown) on the bell that shows the last 5 notifications from the realtime store. Each notification shows type icon, message, and timestamp. The dropdown uses the existing .notif-dropdown CSS classes in style.css.

4. In panel/web/admin/src/components/TheTopbar.vue: Change the bell click behavior - instead of emitting 'open-notifications', make the bell open the dropdown on hover AND navigate to notifications page on click.

5. Create panel/web/admin/src/views/NotificationsView.vue - a dedicated page showing all notifications (from realtime store). Show type, message, timestamp, read/unread state. Allow marking individual as read. Show empty state when no notifications.
6. In panel/web/admin/src/router/index.ts: Add route { path: 'notifications', name: 'notifications', component: () => import('@/views/NotificationsView.vue') } in the AppShell children.
7. In panel/web/admin/src/layouts/AppShell.vue: Update handleNotifications() to navigate to { name: 'notifications' } instead of { name: 'tickets' }.

8. Add i18n keys for notifications page: 'nav.notifications', 'notifications.title', 'notifications.empty', 'notifications.mark_read', 'notifications.mark_all_read' in en/fa/zh in panel/web/admin/src/i18n.ts
acceptance_criteria: No duplicate language switcher - only sidebar has lang selector
Bell shows badge with unread count when > 0
Hovering on bell shows dropdown with recent notifications
Notifications page displays all notifications from realtime store
findings: All verification passed: vue-tsc --noEmit clean, vite build succeeds, vitest run passes (7 tests). The existing .notif-dropdown styles in style.css are global (non-scoped) but since we used scoped styles in TheTopbar.vue for the dropdown, both can coexist. The Locale type import was removed from TheTopbar since we no longer need it (lang switcher removed).
